sourceRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
sourceRefs.title: 2022 Nature Reviews Genetics histone PTM cause/consequence review PDF

paperRefs.title: Histone post-translational modifications - cause and consequence of genome function

rubricProfile.purpose: General refs-first rubric for grading whether a histone PTM is a cause, consequence, reinforcement mechanism, or memory/bookmark of a genomic process.
appliesTo: transcription, recombination, DNA repair, replication, and genome topology claims
research papers that infer causal function from PTM localization, perturbation, or chromatin-state dynamics

claims.text: Histone PTMs should be classified by causal role: a mark can directly or indirectly instruct a DNA-templated process, be deposited as a consequence of that process, reinforce an active state, or bookmark a prior event for memory-like propagation.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 1, Abstract; PDF page 2, introductory framework before Histone PTMs in transcription; Fig. 2
locatorRefs.summary: The review frames PTMs as direct or indirect causes, consequences of DNA-templated processes, reinforcement mechanisms, or bookmarks/records of genomic activity.

locatorRefs.locator: Text lines near opening abstract and Fig. 2 legend
locatorRefs.summary: Bounded text extraction preserves the cause/consequence/reinforcement/bookmarking vocabulary and the direct-versus-indirect mechanism figure legend.

risks.summary: The review provides a general framework, not a single executable statistical test; downstream artifacts must connect each research claim to concrete perturbation, timing, and locality evidence.

claims.text: A PTM mechanism should be separated into writer deposition, reader or effector recognition, eraser removal, and the dynamic balance between writer and eraser activities; perturbing any one component is not automatically equivalent to perturbing the mark itself.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 1, introduction to writers/readers/erasers; PDF pages 2-3, Fig. 2 legend
locatorRefs.summary: The review defines writers, readers, and erasers, emphasizes writer-eraser equilibrium, and shows reader/effector-mediated PTM function.

risks.summary: Writer or eraser perturbations can affect non-histone substrates, chromatin complexes, metabolism, and cell state, so mark-level causality needs local or orthogonal evidence.

claims.text: Active-promoter H3K4me3 illustrates the cause/consequence ambiguity: it correlates with transcription and can recruit transcription machinery, but many functional experiments do not support a universal requirement for H3K4me3 in transcription.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 3-4, Histone tail PTMs section; Fig. 3a-b
locatorRefs.summary: The review contrasts H3K4me3 enrichment and TFIID recruitment with evidence that H3K4me3 is not required for most transcription and may reinforce, protect, or remember chromatin states.

risks.summary: Promoter colocalization or broad H3K4me3 association should not be scored as direct causality without timing, local perturbation, reader mechanism, rescue, or independent validation.

claims.text: Enhancer-associated H3K4me1 and H3K27ac show why writer perturbation, mark abundance, and enhancer function must be disentangled: loss of writers can have stronger effects than loss of the mark, and p300 recruitment can activate genes through mechanisms beyond H3K27ac itself.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 4, enhancer H3K4me1/H3K27ac discussion; PDF page 13, Box 2 editing caveats
locatorRefs.summary: The review notes that enhancer writers can be more important than H3K4me1 itself and that dCas9-p300 activation does not prove H3K27ac is the sole causal feature.

risks.summary: A reproduction rubric should require assays that separate enzymatic writer roles, protein-complex scaffolding, non-histone substrates, and the mark's local chromatin effect.

claims.text: Repressive histone marks such as H3K27me3, H2Aub, and H3K9me3 can be instructive, permissive, inherited, or consequential depending on context; loss of a repressive mark can enable transcription factor action without being sufficient for gene activation.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 5-6, Polycomb and H3K9me3 discussion; Fig. 3c
locatorRefs.summary: The review describes H3K27me3/H2Aub and H3K9me3 as context-dependent repressive systems, with examples where mark loss grants permissibility but transcription still requires lineage-specific factors.

risks.summary: A claim that a repressive PTM directly causes silencing should address transcription-factor availability, cell identity, inheritance, and whether the mark follows rather than precedes repression.

claims.text: Histone core and lateral-surface PTMs are stronger candidates for direct causality when they perturb histone-DNA or histone-histone contacts, whereas many tail PTMs act indirectly through effector proteins.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 6-8, Histone core modifications section; PDF pages 14-15, Conclusions
locatorRefs.summary: The review argues that core PTMs can directly change nucleosome dynamics, while most tail PTMs generally rely on recruited effectors.

risks.summary: In vitro structural causality should still be connected to matched in vivo locus, cell state, and process-level outcomes before grading a biological claim as strong.

claims.text: Recombination and DNA repair examples require explicit distinction between PTMs that mark an accessible chromatin environment and PTMs that allosterically recruit or choose repair machinery.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 8-10, Histone PTMs in recombination and DNA repair; Fig. 4a-d
locatorRefs.summary: The review contrasts PRDM9-associated hotspot marks and V(D)J H3K4me3-RAG2 function with DNA repair pathway-choice mechanisms involving H4K20me, H2AK15ub, and H3K36me3.

risks.summary: Hotspot enrichment or damage-induced deposition can be association or consequence; a strong causal grade needs reader dependence, timing before the outcome, or perturbation of the local mark-reader axis.

claims.text: Replication and genome-topology claims need separate tests for establishment versus maintenance: PTMs such as H4K20 methylation, H3K9 methylation, and H3K27me3 can stabilize or organize already defined origins and compartments, but correlation with replication timing or compartments is not itself causal evidence.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 10-13, Histone PTMs in replication and genome topology; Fig. 4e; Box 2
locatorRefs.summary: The review describes PTMs in replication initiation, compartmentalization, lamina-associated domains, Polycomb domains, and local dCas9 H3K9me3 writer recruitment.

risks.summary: Replication timing, cell cycle, and 3D architecture can reciprocally change the PTM landscape, so causal direction must be established with acute or local perturbation.

claims.text: Local and temporally controlled PTM editing is a high-value causal test, but variable effects of dCas9, TAL, or tethered modifier systems must be interpreted with controls for targeting efficiency, chromatin accessibility, endogenous PTM context, and non-histone substrates.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 13, Box 2: editing histone PTMs; PDF pages 14-15, Conclusions and future perspectives
locatorRefs.summary: The review treats targeted PTM deposition or removal as a route to causal characterization while listing limitations of CRISPR-Cas-based and modifier-recruitment systems.

risks.summary: Editing tools can fail in heterochromatin, recruit multi-substrate complexes, or require PTM flux; a rubric should require controls rather than treating any local modifier fusion as definitive.

claims.text: Common confounders for histone PTM causality include cell-state shifts, stage or cell-cycle timing, transcription-factor availability, antibody and peak-calling specificity, point-mutation artifacts, global writer or eraser pleiotropy, indirect transcriptional effects, and reciprocal feedback with DNA methylation, replication, repair, or genome topology.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 3, 6, 10, 13-15; H3K4me3 functional caveats, histone mutation caution, replication/topology reciprocity, Box 2, and Conclusions
locatorRefs.summary: The review repeatedly warns that PTM correlations and perturbations are context dependent and can reflect reciprocal networks rather than one-way mark causality.

risks.summary: This claim is a generic audit checklist; applying it to a research paper should record which confounders were actually tested and which remain missing evidence.

diagnostics: This is a review/rubric claim graph encoded in the existing paper-claim-graph schema so it can be reused by the same verifier and artifact view as research-paper claim graphs.
The graph is intentionally refs-first: the PDF and pdftotext extraction remain in workspace files, while each claim carries bounded summaries and page/section locators.
Claims are phrased as transferable causal-evidence criteria, not as hard-coded rules for the 2020 PRDM9 or 2025 SETD1B papers.
notes: Use c1-c2 as the top-level cause/consequence and writer-reader-eraser rubric.
Use c3-c5 for transcription and memory/reinforcement grading.
Use c7 for recombination and DNA repair claims, including PRDM9/DSB-fate style papers.
Use c9-c10 as perturbation, rescue, and confounder gates before promoting a PTM claim to direct-causal-strong.
